151.iwh_en: It was narrated from the mother of Abd al-Rahman ibn Tariq ibn Alqama (may Allah be pleased with her) that the Prophet ﷺ, whenever he entered a room in the house of Ya'la — as identified by Ubayd Allah — would face the Ka'ba and supplicate.
151.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari ibu Abdurrahman bin Tariq bin Alqamah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa Nabi ﷺ, setiap kali memasuki sebuah ruangan di rumah Ya'la — sebagaimana diidentifikasi oleh Ubaidullah — akan menghadap Ka'bah dan berdoa.

152.iwh_en: It was narrated from the mother of Abd al-Rahman ibn Tariq (may Allah be pleased with her) that the Prophet ﷺ, whenever he entered a room in the house of Ya'la — as identified by Ubayd Allah — would face the Ka'ba and supplicate. He said: "When I and Abd Allah ibn Kathir came to that place, we would face the Ka'ba and supplicate."
152.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari ibu Abdurrahman bin Tariq (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa Nabi ﷺ, setiap kali memasuki sebuah ruangan di rumah Ya'la — sebagaimana diidentifikasi oleh Ubaidullah — akan menghadap Ka'bah dan berdoa. Ia berkata: "Ketika aku dan Abdullah bin Katsir datang ke tempat itu, kami menghadap Ka'bah dan berdoa."

153.iwh_en: It was narrated from Safiyya bint Shayba (may Allah be pleased with her) that a woman told her that she heard the Prophet ﷺ between al-Safa and al-Marwa saying: "The sa'y has been prescribed for you, so perform the sa'y."
153.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Shafiyyah binti Syaibah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa seorang wanita memberitahunya bahwa ia mendengar Nabi ﷺ antara Shafa dan Marwah bersabda: "Sa'i telah diwajibkan atas kalian, maka lakukanlah sa'i."

154.iwh_en: It was narrated from a female Companion (may Allah be pleased with her) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ entered upon her and said: "Apply henna. Do any of you leave off applying henna until her hand looks like a man's hand?" She said: "I never left off applying henna until I met Allah the Almighty, even though she was applying it at the age of eighty."
154.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari seorang wanita sahabiyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ masuk menemuinya dan bersabda: "Pakailah henna. Apakah salah seorang dari kalian meninggalkan pemakaian henna sampai tangannya terlihat seperti tangan laki-laki?" Ia berkata: "Aku tidak pernah meninggalkan pemakaian henna sampai aku bertemu Allah Ta'ala, bahkan ia masih memakainya di usia delapan puluh tahun."

155.iwh_en: It was narrated from a female Companion (may Allah be pleased with her) that the Prophet ﷺ came to her while she was in a tent and said: "How good it is, unless there is a dead thing in it." She said: "So I began to search through it."
155.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari seorang wanita sahabiyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa Nabi ﷺ datang menemuinya sementara ia berada dalam sebuah tenda dan bersabda: "Betapa bagusnya, kecuali jika ada bangkai di dalamnya." Ia berkata: "Maka aku mulai mencarinya."

156.iwh_en: It was narrated from a female Companion (may Allah be pleased with her) that she said: "I brought you to the Prophet ﷺ and said: 'May my father and mother be sacrificed for you, O Messenger of Allah — this is Muhammad ibn Hatib.' He spat in your mouth, wiped over your head, supplicated for you, and kept spitting on your hand saying: 'Remove the affliction, O Lord of mankind, and heal — for You are the Healer. There is no healing except Your healing, a healing that leaves no illness behind.' She said: 'By the time I had taken you from him, your hand had healed.'"
156.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari seorang wanita sahabiyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ia berkata: "Aku membawamu kepada Nabi ﷺ dan berkata: 'Semoga ayah dan ibuku menjadi tebusanmu, ya Rasulullah — ini adalah Muhammad bin Hatib.' Beliau meludah di mulutmu, mengusap kepalamu, mendoakanmu, dan terus meludahi tanganmu sambil bersabda: 'Hilangkan penyakit ini, wahai Rabb manusia, sembuhkanlah — karena Engkaulah yang Maha Menyembuhkan. Tidak ada kesembuhan kecuali kesembuhan-Mu, kesembuhan yang tidak meninggalkan penyakit.' Ia berkata: 'Ketika aku membawamu pergi dari sisinya, tanganmu telah sembuh.'"

157.iwh_en: It was narrated from Asma bint Umays (may Allah be pleased with her) that she said: "I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say: 'O Ali, you are to me in the same position as Aaron was to Moses, except that there is no prophet after me.'"
157.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Asma binti Umais (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ia berkata: "Aku mendengar Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Wahai Ali, kedudukanmu terhadapku adalah seperti kedudukan Harun terhadap Musa, kecuali bahwa tidak ada nabi setelahku.'"

158.iwh_en: It was narrated from Asma bint Umays (may Allah be pleased with her) that she said: "When Ja'far was killed, the Prophet ﷺ came to us and said to my mother: 'Put on mourning clothes for three days, then do as you wish.'"
158.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Asma binti Umais (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ia berkata: "Ketika Ja'far terbunuh, Nabi ﷺ datang kepada kami dan berkata kepada ibuku: 'Pakailah pakaian berkabung selama tiga hari, kemudian lakukan apa yang kamu mau.'"

159.iwh_en: It was narrated from Asma bint Umays (may Allah be pleased with her) that she was among those present when the Companions said: "O Messenger of Allah, we suspect you have pleurisy." He said: "That is a disease that Allah the Almighty would not afflict me with. Let everyone in this house take the medicine administered by side — except the uncle of the Messenger of Allah," meaning al-Abbas. Maymuna was administered it that day even though she was fasting, due to the command of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ.
159.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Asma binti Umais (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ia termasuk di antara mereka yang hadir ketika para sahabat berkata: "Ya Rasulullah, kami menduga engkau terkena radang selaput dada." Beliau bersabda: "Itu adalah penyakit yang Allah 'Azza wa Jalla tidak akan menimpakan kepadaku. Biarkan semua orang di rumah ini mengonsumsi obat yang diberikan dari samping — kecuali paman Rasulullah," maksudnya al-Abbas. Maimunah diberikan obat itu pada hari itu meskipun ia sedang berpuasa, karena perintah Rasulullah ﷺ.

160.iwh_en: It was narrated from Asma bint Umays (may Allah be pleased with her) that she said: "O Messenger of Allah, the children of Ja'far are affected by the evil eye — shall I have a ruqya done for them?" He said: "Yes, for if anything were to outrun destiny, it would be the evil eye."
160.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Asma binti Umais (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ia berkata: "Ya Rasulullah, anak-anak Ja'far terkena 'ain (mata jahat) — apakah aku boleh melakukan ruqyah untuk mereka?" Beliau bersabda: "Ya, karena jika ada sesuatu yang bisa mendahului takdir, itu adalah 'ain."

161.iwh_en: It was narrated from a female Companion (may Allah be pleased with her) that she said: "He extended his hand to us and said: 'Take from it.'" She said: "I took it shyly and drank from it. He then said: 'Pass it to your companions.' We said: 'We do not want it.' He said: 'Do not combine hunger with a lie.'" She said: "I said: 'O Messenger of Allah, if one of us says about something she desires: I do not desire it — is that considered a lie?' He said: 'A lie is recorded as a lie until a small lie is recorded as a small lie.'"
161.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari seorang wanita sahabiyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ia berkata: "Beliau mengulurkan tangannya kepada kami dan bersabda: 'Ambillah darinya.'" Ia berkata: "Aku mengambilnya dengan malu-malu dan minum darinya. Beliau kemudian bersabda: 'Serahkan kepada teman-temanmu.' Kami berkata: 'Kami tidak menginginkannya.' Beliau bersabda: 'Jangan menggabungkan lapar dengan dusta.'" Ia berkata: "Aku berkata: 'Ya Rasulullah, jika salah seorang dari kami berkata tentang sesuatu yang ia inginkan: Aku tidak menginginkannya — apakah itu dianggap dusta?' Beliau bersabda: 'Dusta dicatat sebagai dusta sampai dusta kecil dicatat sebagai dusta kecil.'"

162.iwh_en: It was narrated from a female Companion (may Allah be pleased with her) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ entered upon her and she offered him food. He said to her: "Eat." She said: "I am fasting." He said: "The angels send blessings upon a fasting person when food is eaten in his presence, until they finish."
162.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari seorang wanita sahabiyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ masuk menemuinya dan ia menyajikan makanan untuknya. Beliau berkata kepadanya: "Makanlah." Ia berkata: "Aku sedang berpuasa." Beliau bersabda: "Para malaikat bershalawat atas orang yang berpuasa ketika makanan dimakan di hadapannya, hingga mereka selesai."

163.iwh_en: It was narrated from a female Companion (may Allah be pleased with her) that she said: "We offered him food, and some of those present were fasting. The Prophet ﷺ said: 'When food is eaten in the presence of a fasting person, the angels send blessings upon him.'"
163.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari seorang wanita sahabiyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ia berkata: "Kami menyajikan makanan untuknya, dan beberapa orang yang hadir sedang berpuasa. Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Ketika makanan dimakan di hadapan orang yang berpuasa, para malaikat bershalawat atasnya.'"

164.iwh_en: It was narrated from a female Companion (may Allah be pleased with her) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said regarding istihadah: "Menstruate for six or seven days in the knowledge of Allah, then perform ghusl. When you see that you have become pure and are certain of it, pray for twenty-four or twenty-three nights and their days, and fast — for that is sufficient for you. Do this every month as women menstruate and become pure according to the timing of their periods and purification. If you can, delay Dhuhr and hasten Asr, perform ghusl for both, and pray them together."
164.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari seorang wanita sahabiyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda mengenai istihadah: "Haidlah selama enam atau tujuh hari dalam pengetahuan Allah, kemudian mandilah. Ketika kamu melihat bahwa kamu telah suci dan yakin akan hal itu, shalatlah selama dua puluh empat atau dua puluh tiga malam beserta harinya, dan berpuasalah — karena itu cukup bagimu. Lakukan ini setiap bulan sebagaimana wanita-wanita haid dan suci sesuai dengan waktu haid dan kesucian mereka. Jika kamu mampu, akhirkan Dhuhur dan percepat Ashar, mandi untuk keduanya, dan shalatkan keduanya sekaligus."

165.iwh_en: It was narrated from a female Companion (may Allah be pleased with her) that she went to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ and said: "O Messenger of Allah, I am experiencing an unusual and severe bleeding." He said to her: "Use cotton." She said: "It is more than that — it is flowing heavily." He said: "Use a cloth, and count your menstrual period each month as six or seven days in the knowledge of Allah. Then perform ghusl, pray, and fast for twenty-three or twenty-four days. Delay Dhuhr and hasten Asr, perform ghusl for them once, and delay Maghrib and hasten Isha."
165.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari seorang wanita sahabiyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ia pergi kepada Rasulullah ﷺ dan berkata: "Ya Rasulullah, aku mengalami pendarahan yang tidak biasa dan parah." Beliau berkata kepadanya: "Gunakan kapas." Ia berkata: "Lebih dari itu — darahnya mengalir deras." Beliau bersabda: "Gunakan kain, dan hitung masa haidmu setiap bulan sebagai enam atau tujuh hari dalam pengetahuan Allah. Kemudian mandilah, shalatlahlah, dan berpuasalah selama dua puluh tiga atau dua puluh empat hari. Akhirkan Dhuhur dan percepat Ashar, mandi sekali untuk keduanya, dan akhirkan Maghrib dan percepat Isya."

166.iwh_en: It was narrated from a female Companion (may Allah be pleased with her) that she heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ when a man asked him about the most virtuous deeds. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Prayer at its earliest time."
166.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari seorang wanita sahabiyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ia mendengar Rasulullah ﷺ ketika seorang laki-laki bertanya kepadanya tentang amalan yang paling utama. Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: "Shalat di awal waktunya."

167.iwh_en: It was narrated from Umm Kurz al-Khuza'iyya (may Allah be pleased with her) that she said: "A baby boy was brought to the Prophet ﷺ and urinated on him, so he ordered that water be sprinkled on it. Then a baby girl was brought and she urinated on him, so he ordered that it be washed."
167.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Umm Kurz al-Khuza'iyyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ia berkata: "Seorang bayi laki-laki dibawa kepada Nabi ﷺ dan kencing di atasnya, maka beliau memerintahkan agar diperciki air. Kemudian seorang bayi perempuan dibawa dan kencing di atasnya, maka beliau memerintahkan agar dicuci."

168.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu al-Darda (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Let none of you neglect to perform a thousand good deeds for Allah each morning — say 'Subhan Allah wa bihamdihi' one hundred times, for that equals one thousand good deeds. By the will of Allah, he will not commit sins equivalent to that in his day, and whatever other good deeds he does will be in addition."
168.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu al-Darda (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: "Janganlah salah seorang dari kalian melalaikan seribu kebaikan untuk Allah setiap pagi — ucapkan 'Subhanallah wa bihamdihi' seratus kali, karena itu setara dengan seribu kebaikan. Dengan izin Allah, ia tidak akan melakukan dosa yang setara dengan itu di harinya, dan kebaikan lain yang ia lakukan akan menjadi tambahan."

169.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu al-Darda (may Allah be pleased with him) from the Prophet ﷺ that he said: "Whoever removes something from a Muslim's path that harms them, Allah will record a good deed for him. And whoever has a good deed recorded by Him, Allah will admit him into Paradise through it."
169.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu al-Darda (radhiyallahu 'anhu) dari Nabi ﷺ bahwa beliau bersabda: "Barangsiapa menyingkirkan sesuatu dari jalan kaum Muslim yang menyakiti mereka, Allah akan mencatat satu kebaikan untuknya. Dan barangsiapa yang dicatat untuknya satu kebaikan oleh-Nya, Allah akan memasukkannya ke surga karenanya."

170.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu al-Darda (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Allah the Almighty says: 'O son of Adam, do not be incapable of performing four rak'ahs at the beginning of your day — I will suffice you for the rest of it.'"
170.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu al-Darda (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: "Allah Ta'ala berfirman: 'Wahai anak Adam, janganlah kamu tidak mampu melakukan empat rakaat di awal harimu — Aku akan mencukupimu untuk sisanya.'"

171.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu al-Darda (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: "My close friend Abu al-Qasim ﷺ advised me with three things I will never abandon for anything: he advised me to fast three days of every month, to not sleep except after performing the Witr prayer, and to pray the forenoon prayer whether at home or traveling."
171.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu al-Darda (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: "Sahabat karibku Abu al-Qasim ﷺ menasihati aku dengan tiga hal yang tidak akan pernah aku tinggalkan karena alasan apa pun: beliau menasihati aku untuk berpuasa tiga hari setiap bulan, untuk tidak tidur kecuali setelah melaksanakan shalat Witir, dan untuk melaksanakan shalat dhuha baik di rumah maupun dalam perjalanan."

172.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu al-Darda (may Allah be pleased with him) from the Messenger of Allah ﷺ that he said: "Allah has gifted you with a third of your wealth at the time of your death."
172.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu al-Darda (radhiyallahu 'anhu) dari Rasulullah ﷺ bahwa beliau bersabda: "Allah telah menghadiahkan kepada kalian sepertiga harta kalian pada saat kematian kalian."

173.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu al-Darda (may Allah be pleased with him) from the Prophet ﷺ that he said: "Everything decreases, except evil — for it only increases."
173.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu al-Darda (radhiyallahu 'anhu) dari Nabi ﷺ bahwa beliau bersabda: "Segala sesuatu berkurang, kecuali kejahatan — karena ia hanya bertambah."

174.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu al-Darda (may Allah be pleased with him) from the Prophet ﷺ that he said: "The one who mistreats his parents will not enter Paradise, nor the believer in magic, nor the one addicted to wine, nor the one who denies divine decree."
174.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu al-Darda (radhiyallahu 'anhu) dari Nabi ﷺ bahwa beliau bersabda: "Orang yang durhaka kepada orang tuanya tidak akan masuk surga, tidak pula orang yang percaya pada sihir, tidak pula pecandu minuman keras, tidak pula orang yang mengingkari takdir."

175.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu al-Darda (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: "The Messenger of Allah ﷺ made a covenant with us, saying: 'The thing I fear most for you is the misguiding leaders.'"
175.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu al-Darda (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: "Rasulullah ﷺ membuat janji dengan kami, bersabda: 'Hal yang paling aku khawatirkan atas kalian adalah para pemimpin yang menyesatkan.'"

176.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu al-Darda (may Allah be pleased with him) from the Prophet ﷺ that he said: "If Allah were to forgive you for what you do to animals, He would forgive you much."
176.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu al-Darda (radhiyallahu 'anhu) dari Nabi ﷺ bahwa beliau bersabda: "Jika Allah memaafkan kalian atas apa yang kalian lakukan kepada binatang, Dia akan memaafkan kalian banyak."

177.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu al-Darda (may Allah be pleased with him) that a man asked the Prophet ﷺ: "Is this matter already decided, or is it something we are initiating?" He said: "Rather, it is a matter already decided." They said: "Then what is the point of deeds, O Messenger of Allah?" He said: "Every person is facilitated toward what he was created for."
177.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu al-Darda (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa seorang laki-laki bertanya kepada Nabi ﷺ: "Apakah perkara ini sudah ditetapkan, ataukah sesuatu yang kita mulai?" Beliau bersabda: "Bahkan, itu adalah perkara yang sudah ditetapkan." Mereka berkata: "Lalu apa gunanya amal, ya Rasulullah?" Beliau bersabda: "Setiap orang dimudahkan menuju apa yang ia diciptakan untuknya."

178.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu al-Darda (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ described how Allah took the descendants of Adam: from his right shoulder came white offspring like specks of dust, and from his left shoulder came dark offspring like embers. To those on his right He said: "To Paradise — and I do not care." To those on his left He said: "To the Fire — and I do not care."
178.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu al-Darda (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ menggambarkan bagaimana Allah mengambil keturunan Adam: dari bahu kanannya keluar keturunan putih seperti butiran debu, dan dari bahu kirinya keluar keturunan gelap seperti bara api. Kepada mereka yang di kanan Dia berfirman: "Ke surga — dan Aku tidak peduli." Kepada mereka yang di kiri Dia berfirman: "Ke neraka — dan Aku tidak peduli."

179.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu al-Darda (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ said: "O Adam, peace be upon you — arise and send nine hundred and ninety-nine of your offspring to the Fire and one to Paradise." His Companions wept. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ then said: "Raise your heads, for by Him in Whose Hand is my soul, my community among all nations is like a single white hair on the hide of a black bull." And this comforted them.
179.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu al-Darda (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: "Wahai Adam, semoga kesejahteraan tercurah atasmu — bangkitlah dan kirimkan sembilan ratus sembilan puluh sembilan dari keturunanmu ke neraka dan satu ke surga." Para sahabatnya menangis. Rasulullah ﷺ kemudian bersabda: "Angkatlah kepala kalian, karena demi Dia yang jiwaku ada di tangan-Nya, umatku di antara semua umat bagaikan selembar rambut putih di kulit sapi hitam." Dan ini menghibur mereka.

180.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu al-Darda (may Allah be pleased with him) that a servant does not reach the true reality of faith until he knows that what befell him could not have missed him, and what missed him could not have befallen him.
180.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu al-Darda (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa seorang hamba tidak mencapai hakikat iman yang sebenarnya sampai ia mengetahui bahwa apa yang menimpanya tidak mungkin terlewatkan darinya, dan apa yang terlewat darinya tidak mungkin menimpanya.

181.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu al-Darda (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ said: "Even if he commits fornication and even if he steals" — which displeased Abu al-Darda. He went out to announce this to the people, but Umar met him and said: "Go back — if people learn of this, they will rely on it." He went back and informed the Prophet ﷺ, who said: "Umar spoke the truth."
181.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu al-Darda (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: "Meskipun ia berzina dan meskipun ia mencuri" — yang tidak menyenangkan Abu al-Darda. Ia pergi untuk mengumumkan ini kepada orang-orang, tetapi Umar menemuinya dan berkata: "Kembalilah — jika orang-orang mengetahui ini, mereka akan bersandar padanya." Ia kembali dan memberitahu Nabi ﷺ, yang bersabda: "Umar berkata benar."

182.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu al-Darda (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Whoever intentionally misses the Asr prayer until it has passed — his deeds are nullified."
182.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu al-Darda (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: "Barangsiapa dengan sengaja meninggalkan shalat Ashar sampai waktunya berlalu — amalnya menjadi sia-sia."

183.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu al-Darda (may Allah be pleased with her) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Neither the sky has shaded, nor the earth has supported, anyone more truthful in speech than Abu Dharr."
183.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu al-Darda (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: "Langit tidak pernah menaungi dan bumi tidak pernah menanggung siapapun yang lebih jujur dalam berbicara daripada Abu Dzarr."

184.iwh_en: It was narrated from Umm al-Darda (may Allah be pleased with her) that Abu al-Darda said: "I performed eleven prostrations with the Prophet ﷺ, among them the prostration of the surah al-Najm."
184.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Umm al-Darda (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa Abu al-Darda berkata: "Aku melakukan sebelas sujud bersama Nabi ﷺ, di antaranya adalah sujud surah al-Najm."

185.iwh_en: It was narrated from Umm al-Darda (may Allah be pleased with her) from the Prophet ﷺ that he said: "Is any of you unable to recite a third of the Quran in a single night?" Someone said: "Who can manage that?" He said: "Recite 'Say: He is Allah, the One' (Surah al-Ikhlas)."
185.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Umm al-Darda (radhiyallahu 'anha) dari Nabi ﷺ bahwa beliau bersabda: "Apakah ada di antara kalian yang tidak mampu membaca sepertiga Al-Quran dalam satu malam?" Seseorang berkata: "Siapa yang mampu melakukan itu?" Beliau bersabda: "Bacalah 'Katakanlah: Dia adalah Allah, Yang Maha Esa' (Surah al-Ikhlas)."

186.iwh_en: It was narrated from Umm al-Darda (may Allah be pleased with her) that she heard Abu al-Darda say: "The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'The best — or: the heaviest — thing in the Scale on the Day of Resurrection is good character.'"
186.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Umm al-Darda (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ia mendengar Abu al-Darda berkata: "Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Hal terbaik — atau: terberat — dalam timbangan pada Hari Kiamat adalah akhlak yang baik.'"

187.iwh_en: It was narrated from Umm al-Darda (may Allah be pleased with her) that Abu al-Darda said: "Carry me out." So they carried him out. He said: "Sit me up." So they sat him up. He said: "O people, I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say: 'Whoever performs ablution properly and then prays two rak'ahs completing them properly, Allah will give him whatever he asks — whether immediately or later.'" Abu al-Darda said: "O people, beware of turning your gaze during prayer, for there is no prayer for one who turns his gaze. But if you are overpowered in voluntary prayer, do not allow yourselves to be overpowered in the obligatory prayer."
187.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Umm al-Darda (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa Abu al-Darda berkata: "Bawa aku keluar." Maka mereka membawanya keluar. Ia berkata: "Dudukkan aku." Maka mereka mendudukkannya. Ia berkata: "Wahai manusia, aku mendengar Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Barangsiapa berwudhu dengan sempurna kemudian shalat dua rakaat dengan sempurna, Allah akan memberinya apa yang ia minta — baik segera maupun kemudian.'" Abu al-Darda berkata: "Wahai manusia, berhati-hatilah dari memalingkan pandangan dalam shalat, karena tidak ada shalat bagi orang yang memalingkan pandangannya. Namun jika kalian terpaksa melakukannya dalam shalat sunnah, jangan sampai kalian terpaksa melakukannya dalam shalat wajib."

188.iwh_en: It was narrated from Umm al-Darda (may Allah be pleased with her) that the Prophet ﷺ said: "Is any of you unable to recite a third of the Quran in a single night?" They said: "We are too weak and incapable for that." He said: "Indeed, Allah the Almighty has divided the Quran into three parts and made 'Say: He is Allah, the One' one of the three parts of the Quran."
188.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Umm al-Darda (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: "Apakah ada di antara kalian yang tidak mampu membaca sepertiga Al-Quran dalam satu malam?" Mereka berkata: "Kami terlalu lemah dan tidak mampu untuk itu." Beliau bersabda: "Sesungguhnya Allah Ta'ala telah membagi Al-Quran menjadi tiga bagian dan menjadikan 'Katakanlah: Dia adalah Allah, Yang Maha Esa' salah satu dari tiga bagian Al-Quran."

189.iwh_en: It was narrated from Umm al-Darda (may Allah be pleased with her) that she said: "We were with the Messenger of Allah ﷺ discussing what would happen, when the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'If you hear that a mountain has shifted from its place, believe it. But if you hear that a man's character has changed, do not believe it — for he will return to what he was created upon.'"
189.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Umm al-Darda (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ia berkata: "Kami bersama Rasulullah ﷺ membicarakan apa yang akan terjadi, ketika Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Jika kalian mendengar bahwa sebuah gunung telah berpindah dari tempatnya, percayailah. Namun jika kalian mendengar bahwa karakter seseorang telah berubah, jangan percayai — karena ia akan kembali kepada apa yang ia diciptakan di atasnya.'"

190.iwh_en: It was narrated from Umm al-Darda (may Allah be pleased with her) that she said: "Abu al-Darda entered upon me in a state of anger. I said: 'Who has angered you?' He said: 'By Allah, I no longer recognize among them anything from the command of Muhammad ﷺ — except that they pray together.'"
190.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Umm al-Darda (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ia berkata: "Abu al-Darda masuk menemuiku dalam keadaan marah. Aku berkata: 'Siapa yang membuatmu marah?' Ia berkata: 'Demi Allah, aku tidak lagi mengenali dari mereka sesuatu pun dari perintah Muhammad ﷺ — kecuali bahwa mereka shalat berjamaah.'"

191.iwh_en: It was narrated from Umm al-Darda (may Allah be pleased with her) that she said: "Abu al-Darda entered upon me in a state of anger. I said to him: 'What is wrong with you?' He said: 'I no longer recognize anything from the command of Muhammad ﷺ — except the prayer.'"
191.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Umm al-Darda (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ia berkata: "Abu al-Darda masuk menemuiku dalam keadaan marah. Aku berkata kepadanya: 'Ada apa denganmu?' Ia berkata: 'Aku tidak lagi mengenali sesuatu pun dari perintah Muhammad ﷺ — kecuali shalat.'"

192.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu al-Darda (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ vomited and broke his fast. He said: "I met Thawban, the freed slave of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, in the mosque of Damascus and said: 'Abu al-Darda told me that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ vomited and broke his fast.' He said: 'He told the truth — I was the one who poured water for his ablution.'"
192.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu al-Darda (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ muntah dan berbuka puasa. Ia berkata: "Aku bertemu Tsauban, mantan budak Rasulullah ﷺ, di masjid Damaskus dan berkata: 'Abu al-Darda memberitahuku bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ muntah dan berbuka puasa.' Ia berkata: 'Ia berkata benar — akulah yang menuangkan air untuk wudhunya.'"

193.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu al-Darda (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Allah will not gather in the body of a man the dust of fighting in the way of Allah and the smoke of Hellfire. Whoever's feet become dusty in the way of Allah, Allah will forbid the rest of his body from the Fire. Whoever fasts a day in the way of Allah, Allah will distance the Fire from him by a journey of a thousand years for a swift rider. And whoever is wounded in the way of Allah..."
193.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu al-Darda (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: "Allah tidak akan mengumpulkan dalam tubuh seseorang debu dari berjuang di jalan Allah dan asap neraka Jahannam. Siapa pun yang kakinya berdebu di jalan Allah, Allah akan mengharamkan bagian tubuhnya yang lain dari api neraka. Barangsiapa berpuasa sehari di jalan Allah, Allah akan menjauhkan api neraka darinya sejauh perjalanan seribu tahun bagi pengendara yang cepat. Dan barangsiapa yang terluka di jalan Allah..."

194.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu al-Darda (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Companions were on an expedition on a very hot day. Every person had to put his hand on his head due to the extreme heat, and among the group there were no fasting people except the Messenger of Allah ﷺ and Abd Allah ibn Rawaha.
194.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu al-Darda (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa para sahabat sedang dalam sebuah ekspedisi pada hari yang sangat panas. Setiap orang harus meletakkan tangannya di kepalanya karena panasnya yang luar biasa, dan di antara kelompok itu tidak ada yang berpuasa kecuali Rasulullah ﷺ dan Abdullah bin Rawahah.

195.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu al-Darda (may Allah be pleased with him) that he heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say regarding the verse: "Of them is he who wrongs himself" — he said: "The wrongdoer is seized in his standing place — that is the grief and sorrow. 'And of them is he who follows a middle course' — he is held to account with an easy reckoning. 'And of them is he who outstrips others in good deeds' — those are the ones who will enter Paradise without reckoning."
195.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu al-Darda (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia mendengar Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda mengenai ayat: "Di antara mereka ada yang menzalimi dirinya sendiri" — beliau bersabda: "Orang yang zalim diambil pertanggungjawabannya di tempat berdirinya — itulah kesedihan dan duka. 'Dan di antara mereka ada yang pertengahan' — ia dihisab dengan perhitungan yang mudah. 'Dan di antara mereka ada yang berlomba-lomba dalam kebaikan' — mereka adalah orang-orang yang akan masuk surga tanpa hisab."

196.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu al-Darda (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Whoever plants a tree from which no human being or any of Allah's creation eats, it will count for him as charity."
196.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu al-Darda (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: "Barangsiapa menanam pohon yang darinya tidak ada manusia atau makhluk Allah mana pun yang memakan, maka itu akan dihitung sebagai sedekah baginya."

197.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu al-Darda (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "O Abu al-Darda, do not single out the night of Friday for prayer above other nights, and do not single out the day of Friday for fasting above other days."
197.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu al-Darda (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: "Wahai Abu al-Darda, janganlah engkau mengistimewakan malam Jumat untuk qiyamullail di atas malam-malam lainnya, dan janganlah engkau mengistimewakan hari Jumat untuk berpuasa di atas hari-hari lainnya."

198.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu al-Darda (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ said: "Shall I not tell you of something better in degree than prayer, fasting, and charity?" They said: "Certainly." He said: "Reconciling between people. And corrupting the relations between people is the destroyer."
198.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu al-Darda (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: "Tidakkah aku beritahukan kepada kalian sesuatu yang lebih baik derajatnya daripada shalat, puasa, dan sedekah?" Mereka berkata: "Tentu saja." Beliau bersabda: "Mendamaikan orang-orang. Dan merusak hubungan di antara orang-orang adalah penghancur."

199.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu al-Darda (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Whoever hears a statement from a man that he would not want attributed to him — it is a trust, even if that man did not ask him to keep it secret."
199.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu al-Darda (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: "Barangsiapa mendengar suatu pernyataan dari seseorang yang orang tersebut tidak ingin dikaitkan dengannya — itu adalah amanah, meskipun orang itu tidak memintanya untuk merahasiakannya."

200.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu al-Darda (may Allah be pleased with him) from the Prophet ﷺ regarding the verse: "For them is glad tidings in this worldly life and in the Hereafter" — he said: "It is the righteous vision that a Muslim sees or that is seen for him."
200.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu al-Darda (radhiyallahu 'anhu) dari Nabi ﷺ mengenai ayat: "Bagi mereka kabar gembira di kehidupan dunia dan di akhirat" — beliau bersabda: "Itu adalah mimpi yang baik yang seorang Muslim lihat atau yang dilihat untuknya."
